steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, ${d}^{2}+14$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Combine the constants
steps.content: The expression $9+d^2+5$ has two numerical constants and one $d^2$ term. Add the constants: $$9+5=14.$$ The $d^2$ term has no like term to combine with, so it stays as is.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $d^2+45$ — would need $9+5=45$, but $9+5=14$, not 45. Eliminated.
  • B) $d^2+14$ — matches $9+5=14$ exactly.
  • C) $d^2+4$ — would require subtracting, but the expression adds the constants. Eliminated.
  • D) $d^2-14$ — the sign is wrong; $9+5$ is positive 14. Eliminated.
That matches Choice B — ${d}^{2}+14$.
